programme.title: Ray Charles live at North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: Le North Sea Jazz Festival est connu pour sa large programmation, allant du jazz traditionnel de la Nouvelle-Orléans, du swing, du bop, de la fusion et de l'avant-garde au blues, au gospel, au funk, à la soul, au R&B, au hip-hop et au latin.

programme.title: Comblain-La-Tour '65: John Coltrane
programme.desc: Ces trois concerts distincts mettent en avant l'évolution du talent créateur de Coltrane, en tant qu'innovateur du hard bop alors qu'il faisait partie du Miles Davis Quartet en 1960 ou comme chef d'orchestre accompli en 1961.

programme.title: BIRDtv
programme.desc: Le club et restaurant BIRD propose principalement du jazz mais présente également des artistes de soul, funk, hip-hop et musique électronique. Le nom BIRD est une référence au surnom du musicien de jazz new-yorkais légendaire Charlie Parker.

programme.title: Gregory Porter at the Blue Note Festival
programme.desc: Retrouvez le chanteur américain Gregory Porter et son groupe au festival Blue Note à Paris.

programme.title: DJAZZ Portrait

programme.title: Great Guitarists
programme.desc: Une sélection de performances inoubliables de certains des guitaristes les plus emblématiques et influents de l'histoire du jazz. Avec Wes Montgomery, Joe Pass, Jim Hall, Attila Zoller ou encore Sonny Sharrock.

programme.title: Tony Bennett 4:3
programme.desc: La magie de Tony Bennett, l'un des crooners favoris de l'histoire de la musique. Enregistré en 1974, ce concert enchanteur met en lumière la virtuosité vocale et le charisme incomparables de Bennett alors qu'il se trouvait au sommet de son art.

programme.title: Great Jazz Pianists
programme.desc: Prenez part à un périple remarquable dans l'univers du piano jazz à la rencontre de certains des musiciens les plus emblématiques et influents de l'histoire du genre.

programme.title: Great Jazz Pianists
programme.desc: Prenez part à un périple remarquable dans l'univers du piano jazz à la rencontre de certains des musiciens les plus emblématiques et influents de l'histoire du genre.

programme.title: The Brothers Four: Live in Comblain-la-Tour
programme.desc: Ce quartet folk-pop a fait figure de précurseur dès sa formation en 1957 par quatre membres d'une fraternité de l'Université de Washington. Les harmonies fluides, chaudes et luxuriantes des Brothers Four leur ont rapidement permis de sortir du lot.

programme.title: Ryan Porter & the West Coast Get Down -- BIRD
programme.desc: Saxophonist Kamasi Washington and trombonist Ryan Porter co-founded West Coast Get Down, a musical collective fusing jazz and funk with hip-hop. The musicians join forces and take the vibes of Los Angeles to jazz club BIRD in Rotterdam.

programme.title: The Garifuna Collektive: WOMEX 2018
programme.desc: In 2018, WOMEX was held in Las Palmas, Gran Canaria. One of its showcase participants, The Garífuna Collective, carries the legacy of Andy Palacio.

programme.title: Kim So Ra: WOMEX 2018
programme.desc: In 2018, WOMEX was held in Las Palmas, Gran Canaria. One of its showcase participants, Korean percussionist Kim So Ra, has played the hourglass-shaped, two-headed drum.

programme.title: Audio Obscura x Rijksmuseum
programme.desc: A collaboration between Audio Obscura and the Dutch Rijksmuseum on an experimental project blending electronic music with the rich culture, heritage and history of the Dutch museum.

programme.title: Moonlight Benjamin: WOMEX 2018
programme.desc: In 2018, WOMEX was held in Las Palmas, Gran Canaria. One of its showcase participants, Moonlight Benjamin, blends together Voodoo blues, Haitian rock and Creole roll.

programme.title: Filo Machado - New Year's Eve Special
programme.desc: Singer, composer, instrumentalist, arranger and producer Filo Machado performs Latin jazz in a New Year's Eve special presented by Da Pá Virada Sessions and Stingray.

programme.title: Christine and the Queens at Vieilles Charrues
programme.desc: In 2018, Christine and the Queens shortened her name to Chris and hit the international scene with a self-titled album. She inspires her audience in this concert at Les Vieilles Charrues in Carhaix, France, to dance all night long.

programme.title: Reading and Leeds Festival 2022
programme.desc: A look at the Reading and Leeds Festivals, a pair of annual music festivals that take place in Reading and Leeds, England. The events happen on the bank holiday weekend in August, and share the same bill.

programme.title: Beth Ditto : Lille 2017
programme.desc: Four days after her performance at the Bataclan, the singer Beth Ditto performed her first solo album, `Fake Sugar', on the Lille stage.

programme.title: Jazz in Duketown
programme.desc: The band plays a mix of various styles, inspired by funk, jazz, dub, rock, and others. The ensemble made its debut in 2010 and has played at numerous festivals. Their December 2010 performance with former backing musicians of Afrobeat founder Fela K.

programme.desc: Since the German bands Modeselektor (Gernot Bronsert and Sebastian Szary) and Apparat (Sascha Ring) joined forces in 2009, they have been hugely successful as the electronic music supergroup Moderat.

programme.title: Malta Jazz Festival

programme.title: Da Pá Virada Sessions
programme.desc: Vocalist Mariana Aydar performs Brazilian pieces alongside Cosme Vieira and Feeh Silva. Mariana Aydar was born to a family of talented musicians and specialises in Brazilian popular music.

programme.title: Invisible World: WOMEX 2018
programme.desc: In 2018, WOMEX was held in Las Palmas, Gran Canaria. One of its participating bands, Invisible World, is based at the jazz crossroads where East European and Mediterranean influences merge and caper off in new directions.

programme.title: Da Pá Virada Sessions
programme.desc: Vocalist Vanessa Moreno, bassist Fi Marostica, keyboard player Tiago Costa, and drummer Jonatas Sansao pay tribute to Elis Regina by performing a range of pieces from her repertoire.

programme.title: The Hermes Experiment

programme.title: Mestrinho - Da Pá Virada Sessions
programme.desc: Mestrinho is seen in performance with Cainã Cavalcante on guitar, Alex Buck on drums, and Carlinhos Noronha on bass.

programme.title: ParookaVille
programme.desc: Parookaville is a city that comes to life once a year to keep the legend of Bill Parooka alive. It was started as an artistic, free-flowing, wild, and musical place where people could connect with their pure selves.

programme.title: Alfa Jazz Festival Lviv, Ukraine
programme.desc: Concert by pianist Kenny Barron at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Lviv, Ukraine, in 2019. He was accompanied by Kiyoshi Kitagawa on bass, Jonathan Blake on drums, Marcus Strickland on tenor saxophone and Riley Mulherkar on trumpet.

programme.title: Frequencerz Live at Airbeat One Festival
programme.desc: Relive the Frequencerz set on the hardstyle stage Qdance at the Airbeat One Festival in Germany in July 2017.

programme.desc: FORQ performs at the Mondriaan Jazz Festival, 2017.

programme.desc: Profiling Jeditah, an experimental songwriter, producer and singer from Amsterdam. She works with Brendan Jan Walsh on cello and Jordy Pama on synths and electronics.

programme.desc: Experience the incredible richness of our world's music in this performance by the unparalleled Afro-Cuban pianist Omar Sosa! A celebration of rhythm, this concert from the French Ramatuelle Jazz Festival promises an intense mystical experience!

programme.desc: The group Altin Gün came to existence when bassist Jasper Verhulst made an inspiring journey to Turkey. After his show in Istanbul, he felt greatly attracted to the Turkish sound from the 1970s.
